description: 48M, LDL 162, no ASCVD, no diabetes, ASCVD risk 6.1%. Below ACC/AHA's 7.5% threshold for COR I statin recommendation. Borderline risk zone (5-7.5%) where statin may be considered (COR IIb) with risk enhancers.

actions.label: Assess for ASCVD risk-enhancing factors
actions.rationale: Patient falls in the borderline risk category (5-7.5%). ACC/AHA COR IIb, LOE B-R: statin may be considered if risk enhancers are present (family history of premature ASCVD, LDL ≥160, metabolic syndrome, CKD, chronic inflammatory conditions, high-risk ethnicity, elevated Lp(a), apoB, hsCRP, ABI <0.9, TG ≥175). LDL 162 is itself a risk enhancer (≥160).

actions.label: Consider coronary artery calcium (CAC) scoring
actions.rationale: CAC score can help refine risk in the borderline category. A CAC of 0 supports deferral of statin; CAC >0 (especially ≥100 or ≥75th percentile) supports initiation.

actions.label: Emphasize therapeutic lifestyle changes
actions.rationale: Regardless of statin decision, lifestyle modifications (diet, exercise, weight management) are foundational. LDL 162 and TC 224 warrant dietary counseling.

contraindications.label: Routine statin initiation without further risk assessment
contraindications.rationale: ASCVD risk 6.1% is below the 7.5% COR I threshold. ACC/AHA does not recommend routine statin at this risk level without risk enhancer assessment.
